description_long: This object is a traditional stone water well with a circular base constructed from dark, unevenly shaped stones, giving it a rustic appearance. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of overlapping wooden shingles, supported by two vertical wooden posts. A central, slightly twisted wooden beam runs vertically through the middle, likely serving as a support or for mounting a pulley mechanism, though no pulley or bucket is visible. The roof shingles are irregularly cut, adding to the handcrafted look, and the wood appears weathered. The well's opening is wide and unobstructed, with a thick stone rim. There are no visible decorative elements or additional mechanisms attached to the structure.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden shingle roof and a central wooden support beam. The well features a wide stone rim and weathered wood construction, with no visible pulley or bucket.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pitched wooden roof, central twisted wooden support, and the circular stone well opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the wooden roof shingles and the thickness of the stone wall, with a wooden beam extending outwards.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the pitched roof and central support, but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the roof shingles' texture and the stone wall, with another wooden beam visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high, forming the main body of the well.
